skill.name: lark-markdown
skill.description: Use Lark CLI to create, fetch, and overwrite native Markdown files stored in Lark Drive.
skill.summary: Manage native Markdown files in Lark Drive with create, fetch, and overwrite commands.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and path-traversal hits were mostly false positives from Markdown code spans, CLI examples, and relative documentation links. The skill still has medium risk because it instructs agents to run lark-cli commands that read local Markdown files, write local output files, create cloud files, overwrite cloud files, and may grant full_access after bot-created files.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Commands Modify Lark Drive Content
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run lark-cli markdown create, fetch, and overwrite commands. These commands are legitimate for the skill, but they can create or replace remote Drive Markdown files and should require clear user intent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and shortcut routing clearly instruct use of lark-cli for remote create and overwrite operations. This is expected behavior, but it has real cloud-side effects.

medium_findings.title: Local Markdown File Read and Write Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill supports reading local Markdown through --file, @file, or stdin and saving fetched Drive content to local paths with optional overwrite. This can expose local file contents or overwrite local files if used without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly allows local file input, stdin input, local output paths, and overwrite mode. The risk is contextual rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Full Access Grant After Bot-Created Files
medium_findings.description: When a Markdown file is created as a bot, the CLI may attempt to grant the current CLI user full_access. This is disclosed, but it changes permissions and should be visible to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced section documents automatic full_access granting after bot-created files. It also reports status and warns against owner transfer, so the concern is permission sensitivity, not hidden escalation.

low_findings.title: Static Ruby Backtick Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code markers, fenced command examples, and table formatting. No Ruby backtick execution code was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown documentation, and the referenced characters are formatting around command names and flags. I found no executable Ruby source or shell interpolation logic.

low_findings.title: Relative Documentation Links Trigger Path Traversal Heuristic
low_findings.description: The path traversal detections are relative links to neighboring skill documentation, such as lark-shared and lark-drive. They do not perform filesystem access by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The ../ sequences appear in Markdown links and references, not path handling code. They may still indicate a cross-skill dependency, but not path traversal execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Entropy Alerts Are Not Supported By Context
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and high-entropy alerts appear to be heuristic false positives in Markdown text. No cryptographic operation, encoded payload, binary blob, or encrypted content was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain normal description and heading text. I found no evidence of weak crypto use or obfuscated payloads in those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: lark-cli Markdown Operations With Side Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented command pattern lark-cli markdown +create, +fetch, and +overwrite can affect cloud Drive content and local files. It should only run after the target file token, path, and overwrite intent are clear.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is intentionally documented and useful, but it invokes an external CLI and can change user data. That makes it a dangerous pattern requiring user confirmation, not a malicious indicator.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable way to work with native Markdown files in Lark Drive. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through lark-cli commands for creating, fetching, and overwriting Markdown content.
actual_capabilities: Creates native .md files in Lark Drive from inline content, stdin, or a local Markdown file.
Fetches Markdown content from Lark Drive by file token.
Saves fetched Markdown to a local file or directory when an output path is provided.
Overwrites existing Drive Markdown files with inline content, stdin, or a local .md file.
Explains when to use Lark Drive tools instead of this Markdown-specific skill.
Documents dry-run examples for previewing underlying requests before execution.
limitations: It only covers native Markdown files stored as Drive files, not Lark docx documents.
It depends on lark-cli and the shared Lark authentication setup being available.
It does not handle rename, move, delete, search, permissions, or comments for Drive files.
It requires explicit .md suffixes for file names and local file paths.

use_cases.title: Publish Team Markdown Notes
use_cases.description: Create a native Markdown file in Lark Drive from a local README or release note.

use_cases.title: Review Existing Drive Markdown
use_cases.description: Fetch a Drive Markdown file by token and inspect or save the content locally.

use_cases.title: Update Shared Markdown Assets
use_cases.description: Overwrite an existing Lark Drive Markdown file after confirming the file token and new content.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Markdown File
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a native Markdown file in Lark Drive named README.md with this content: # Project Notes

prompt_templates.title: Fetch Markdown Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the Lark Drive Markdown file with token boxcnxxxx and show me the Markdown content.

prompt_templates.title: Save Remote Markdown Locally
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the Markdown file with token boxcnxxxx and save it to ./downloads/README.md. Ask before overwriting anything.

prompt_templates.title: Overwrite After Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ./README.md to overwrite the Drive Markdown file with token boxcnxxxx. First confirm the target token, local file path, and overwrite intent.

output_examples.input: Create README.md in Lark Drive from inline Markdown content.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the file name, runs the create shortcut, and reports the returned Drive file token and size.

output_examples.input: Fetch a Markdown file by file token and save it locally.
output_examples.output: The assistant retrieves the Markdown file, saves it to the requested path, and reports the saved location and file size.

output_examples.input: Overwrite an existing Drive Markdown file from a local .md file.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms overwrite intent, runs the overwrite shortcut, and reports the new version number.

best_practices: Confirm the Lark file token before fetching or overwriting Drive content.
Use dry-run when the target, identity, or permission impact is unclear.
Ask before using overwrite flags or saving fetched content over an existing local file.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for Lark docx imports or rich document editing.
Do not overwrite a Drive Markdown file without explicit user confirmation.
Do not pass broad or unverified local paths as Markdown input or output targets.

faq.question: What type of files does this skill manage?
faq.answer: It manages native Markdown files stored as regular files in Lark Drive.

faq.question: Can it import Markdown as a Lark docx document?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to use the Lark Drive import workflow for docx conversion.

faq.question: Does it require lark-cli?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata requires lark-cli and relies on shared Lark authentication setup.

faq.question: Can it read a local Markdown file?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports local .md input through --file, @file, or stdin, depending on the command.

faq.question: Can it overwrite existing files?
faq.answer: Yes. It can overwrite remote Drive Markdown files and can overwrite local output files when requested.

faq.question: What should users confirm before running it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm the file token, local file path, command identity, and any overwrite or permission impact.
